chunks.text: The Jewish religion has a history of 3,500 years.
It is also the root of Muslim and Christian religions.
The stories of the Old Testament are among the most widely read of humanity's myth.
Noah's Ark, Moses leading the Exodus, David and Goliath.
These stories form the principal foundation of Western culture.
The history of the Jewish people has also been a history of persecution.
Exile by ancient Rome, discrimination in medieval Europe, the twentieth - century Holocaust.
Extensive discrimination continued.
Yet the Jews continued to bear and keep their own culture and faith.
Worship at the synagogue, the Sabbath, the Passover.
Tradition was passed down across generations.
Jewish culture, which values knowledge, produced many outstanding figures.
Einstein, Freud, Kafka, Spielberg.
Scholarship, art, business, science.
In every field, the Jewish contribution should be credit.
But today's Israeli - Palestinian conflict is a serious problem.
Since the founding of Israel in 1948, the conflict between the two has continued.
Both sides have justified claims, and both have victim.
Religion and ethnicity hold the power to generate both love and hatred.
Rather than exclude others, to choose coexistence.
That is the goal of a civilized society.
Religion can be a tool that divides people, or a tool that unify them.
That choice lies in the hands of each believer.
